ambiguities.description: Asset types and categories (physical, digital, IT, fleet, equipment, facilities)
Asset identity and tagging (barcode, QR, RFID, serial, asset tag, auto-generated)
Locations and custody chain (site, building, room, person, department, transfer workflow)
Assignment, check-out, and check-in model (reservation, approval, duration, overdue)
Maintenance schedules and work orders (preventive, corrective, vendor-managed, SLA)
Depreciation and lifecycle model (straight-line, usage-based, disposal, salvage)
Inspections and condition tracking (frequency, criteria, pass/fail, remediation)
Inventory audits and reconciliation (cycle counts, full audits, variance handling)
Integrations with procurement, accounting, ITSM, or fleet management systems
Permissions and access control (who can view, assign, transfer, dispose, audit)
Reporting and analytics (utilization, maintenance cost, depreciation, compliance)
MVP asset class boundary — what asset types are in scope for first release

questions.question: What asset types must be tracked in MVP?
What identity and tagging method is required?
What assignment and custody model is required?
Are maintenance schedules and work orders required?
Is depreciation and lifecycle tracking required?
What inspection and audit model is required?
Must this integrate with procurement, accounting, or ITSM systems?
What reporting and analytics are required?

assumptions.assumption: IT equipment and physical devices are the core MVP asset types
Barcode or QR tagging is sufficient for MVP
Simple check-out/check-in with person-level custody is acceptable initially
Preventive maintenance with simple schedules is sufficient for MVP

suggested_closures.decision: Asset management construction will start with IT equipment and physical devices using barcode/QR tagging, person-level custody, and simple preventive maintenance schedules.
suggested_closures.rationale: A focused asset class and conservative custody model reduce initial complexity while preserving principal control over asset assignment and lifecycle.

seed_tasks.title: Define asset types and categories
seed_tasks.transformation: Document all asset types, categories, and which are in MVP.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every asset type has a category and a defined lifecycle stage model.

seed_tasks.title: Define identity and tagging strategy
seed_tasks.transformation: Document tagging method, label format, and how asset IDs are generated.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every asset has a unique, scannable identifier.

seed_tasks.title: Define custody and transfer workflow
seed_tasks.transformation: Document how assets are assigned, transferred, checked out, checked in, and returned.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every transfer has an authorized actor and a documented chain of custody.

seed_tasks.title: Define maintenance and work order model
seed_tasks.transformation: Document maintenance types, schedules, work order flow, and vendor involvement.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every maintainable asset has a schedule and a responsible party.

seed_tasks.title: Define inspection and audit policy
seed_tasks.transformation: Document inspection frequency, criteria, and how audit variance is handled.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every asset type has an inspection frequency and a variance resolution path.

seed_tasks.title: Define MVP asset class boundary
seed_tasks.transformation: Explicitly list asset types in MVP and what is out of scope.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: MVP scope document exists with explicit inclusions and exclusions.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Scope is achievable and every excluded asset type is consciously deferred.

residuals.description: Asset types for MVP are not defined.
Identity and tagging method is not decided.
Custody and transfer model is not established.
Maintenance and work order model is undefined.
Depreciation and lifecycle model is not decided.
Inspection and audit policy is undocumented.
